title: Lesson 294
transcript: you We've talked through all sorts of different risk management terms. I've got this little video here that will summarize all of them, put these terms together so you can kind of see how they fit together. So let's start with a threat agent. What's a threat agent? A threat agent is something that's going to cause a threat. So an attacker, a hurricane, a fire. These are threat agents. Now, threat agents give rise to threats. And if you recall, a threat is any potential danger. And threats exploit vulnerabilities, because a vulnerability is a weakness that exists. And vulnerabilities lead to risk. And a risk can damage an asset. An asset is anything of value to the organization. And this causes some sort of impact or exposure. What's an impact or an exposure? Well, it's loss of reputation, monetary, loss of confidentiality of data, a breach, loss of integrity. Any of these can be an exposure or an impact. And that leads to how do we counter this? How do we break this chain? How do we prevent these threats from exercising vulnerabilities and causing risks and so forth? Well, we can implement safeguards or countermeasures. And as you recall, safeguards are the controls that we put in place to try and prevent a risk from happening, are directive, deterrent, and preventative controls. And countermeasures are the controls that we put in place to detect and react or correct risks that occurred. So those are our detective, corrective, recovery, and compensating controls. And if we put these in place, they can help to reduce the threat agents, the threats that might occur to the involvement, and we can even, by putting in place controls, remove vulnerabilities. That's a quick summary of some of the key terminology within risk management.
